big_o_cheat_sheet.overall_accuracy: HIGH — mostly correct with minor caveats

issues.expected: O(b^d) worst-case, O(E log V) in typical form; O(E) is overly optimistic even with a perfect heuristic
issues.recommendation: Change to 'O(E log V) typical, O(b^d) worst' or 'O((V+E) log V)'

issues.expected: Correct — standard selection sort is not stable (though can be modified)
Correct — hash tables don't support indexed access

orphaned_sections.header_text: "This appendix contains supplementary material from chapters that were integrated into other sections."
orphaned_sections.assessment: TRUE BUT MISLEADING — these are not just 'supplementary material.' They are FULL, UNEDITED OLD CHAPTER DRAFTS that have been ENTIRELY SUPERSEDED by completely different chapters with the same numbers.

section_1.label: Section 1 (lines 77-323)
section_1.claimed_chapter: Chapter 16 — Algorithm Design and Running Times
section_1.current_chapter_16: Minimum Spanning Trees (Part IV) — COMPLETELY DIFFERENT TOPIC
section_1.content_actually_about: Complexity analysis (Big-O/Big-Omega/Big-Theta), algorithm design techniques, benchmarking in Go, role of data structures in efficiency
section_1.current_mapping: Would map to Part I (Complexity Analysis) + Part VI (Algorithmic Paradigms) in current structure
section_1.value: Low — superseded by current Chapters 2 (Complexity Analysis), 23-28 (Algorithmic Paradigms)

section_2.label: Section 2 (lines 325-911)
section_2.current_chapter_18: Matchings in Bipartite Graphs (Part IV) — COMPLETELY DIFFERENT TOPIC
section_2.content_actually_about: Divide and Conquer, Dynamic Programming, Greedy Algorithms, Backtracking, Branch-and-Bound, Problem Reduction — all with Go code
section_2.current_mapping: Would map to Part VI (Algorithmic Paradigms, current Chapters 23-28)
section_2.value: Low — superseded by current Chapters 23-28 which are more concise and follow the style guide

section_3.label: Section 3 (lines 913-1101)
section_3.current_chapter_19: Basic Sorting Algorithms (Part V) — COMPLETELY DIFFERENT TOPIC
section_3.content_actually_about: Generic graph representation with Go 1.18+ generics, iterative DFS, BFS, bidirectional search, IDDFS
section_3.current_mapping: Would map to current Chapter 13 (Graph Traversal Algorithms, Part IV)
section_3.value: Low — the old content uses Go 1.18+ generics which IS still relevant, but is superseded by current Chapter 13 which covers the same topics more concisely
section_3.internal_inconsistency: Contains headings '21.4.1. Bidirectional Search' and '21.4.2. Iterative Deepening DFS (IDDFS)' — references an old Chapter 21 numbering within its own content

section_4.label: Section 4 (lines 1103-1283)
section_4.current_chapter_20: Advanced Sorting Algorithms (Part V) — COMPLETELY DIFFERENT TOPIC
section_4.content_actually_about: Generic Dijkstra with container/heap, heap staleness check, Bellman-Ford for negative weights
section_4.current_mapping: Would map to current Chapter 14 (Single-Source Shortest Paths, Part IV)

links_check.internal_markdown_links: NONE — the appendix contains no Markdown links to chapters
links_check.alert_shortcode_references: Inline text references to chapters (e.g., 'Chapter 16', 'Chapter 18') but no clickable links — no link issues

recommendations.overall: KEEP THE BIG-O CHEAT SHEET, DELETE OR ARCHIVE THE ORPHANED CHAPTERS

detailed.rationale: Useful quick reference. Keep the tables, fix the A* complexity inaccuracy.
detailed.surgery: Keep lines 1-75 (frontmatter + Big-O Cheat Sheet + trailing '---'), delete everything after

detailed.rationale: Superseded by current Chapters 2, 23-28. The content is longer than current chapters and doesn't follow the style guide (has redundant pseudocode alongside Go code). No readers will arrive here expecting this content.
Superseded by current Part VI chapters (23-28). Same issues as Section 1.
Superseded by current Chapter 13. Contains internal numbering inconsistencies (references '21.4.x').

detailed.item: If there is concern about losing Go 1.18+ generics code for graph traversal/Dijkstra
detailed.rationale: The generic approaches in Sections 3 and 4 use Go 1.18+ generics, which is a more modern approach than what current Ch 13-14 might use. However, the content is still duplicated. Could extract the generic patterns into a separate reference file or code snippets directory if valuable.

strengths: Philosophy section (brevity, intuition, Go idiomatic, practical, honest)
Concise at 43 lines — fits on one screen

docs_index.assessment: FUNCTIONAL but could be improved slightly
weaknesses: Very minimal — just a list of links with no context about the book
No link to the Appendix (which is a top-level doc in the sidebar)
docs_index.recommendation: KEEP but consider adding: (1) a brief context sentence about what the book covers, (2) a link to the Appendix page for discoverability
optional_improvements: Add a sentence: 'The complete reference for data structures and algorithms in Go.'
Add: '- [Appendix: Big-O Cheat Sheet & Supplementary Material](/docs/appendix/)'

cross_file_issues.appendix_vs_chapter_numbering_conflict: CRITICAL — The appendix claims chapters 16, 18, 19, 20 contain specific algorithm/complexity content, but the actual book has completely different chapters with those numbers. Any reader navigating from the appendix to the TOC will be confused.
cross_file_issues.how_to_use_references_appendix: how-to-use-dsag.md line 94 correctly links to the appendix. This link will remain valid if the appendix is trimmed.

critical_findings: Appendix is 56KB (1283 lines) — the largest file in the project by far
~90% of appendix content (lines 77-1283) is ORPHANED old chapter drafts with a numbering scheme that no longer exists
The orphaned sections are labeled 'Chapter 16', '18', '19', '20' but actual Chapters 16/18/19/20 cover completely different topics
Only the Big-O Cheat Sheet (lines 16-74, ~340 words) is valuable supplementary material
Big-O Cheat Sheet is mostly accurate (one minor issue: A* complexity listed as O(E) is too optimistic)
Landing page and docs index are both clean and functional
summary.primary_recommendation: Trim appendix.md to only the Big-O Cheat Sheet (keep lines 1-75, delete lines 76-1283). This reduces the file from 56KB to ~3KB.
summary.secondary_recommendation: Minor A* fix in the Big-O table. Minor optional improvements to docs/_index.md.
